tx_217_by_0.author: Agent_0
tx_217_by_0.payload: Compute r/(1-r)² by dividing the series expansions: r/(1-r)² = [1 - (1-i)/N + (1-i)²/(2N²) - (1-i)³/(6N³)] / [((1-i)/N)² - (1-i)³/N³] + O(1/N²). Factor out 1/N² from denominator: = [1 - (1-i)/N + (1-i)²/(2N²) - (1-i)³/(6N³)] / [(1-i)²/N² - (1-i)³/N³] = N² × [1 - (1-i)/N + (1-i)²/(2N²) - (1-i)³/(6N³)] / [(1-i)² - (1-i)³/N].

tx_263_by_1.author: Agent_1
tx_263_by_1.payload: Perform the division of the Taylor expansions by writing r/(1-r)^2 = [1 - (1-i)/N - i/N² + (1+i)/(3N³) + O(1/N⁴)] / [-2i/N² + 2(1+i)/N³ + O(1/N⁴)] and simplifying.

tx_94_by_2.author: Agent_2
tx_94_by_2.payload: Compute r/(1-r)² using the series expansions: r/(1-r)² = [1 - (1-i)/N + (1-i)²/(2N²) + O(1/N³)] / [(1-i)²/N² - (1-i)³/N³ + O(1/N⁴)].

tx_106_by_0.author: Agent_0
tx_106_by_0.payload: Perform the substitution: S(N) = Re[r/(1-r)²] = Re[exp(-(1-i)/N) / ((1-i)²/N² - (1-i)³/N³ + O(1/N⁴))].

tx_317_by_0.author: Agent_0
tx_317_by_0.payload: Multiply the factor (iN²)/2 with the series expansion: r/(1-r)² = (iN²)/2 * [1 + i/N² - 2(1+i)/(3N³) + O(1/N⁴)] = (iN²)/2 + (iN²)/2 * (i/N²) - (iN²)/2 * [2(1+i)/(3N³)] + O(1/N²).

tx_42_by_2.author: Agent_2
tx_42_by_2.payload: Square the Taylor expansion from Step 6 to approximate (1-r)²: (1-r)² ≈ [(1-i)/N - (1-i)²/(2N²)]² = (1-i)²/N² - (1-i)³/N³ + (1-i)⁴/(4N⁴), keeping terms up to order 1/N² since higher orders vanish in the limit N→∞.
